inference_unit.notations: - $\xi$: Ginzburg-Landau coherence length of the superconducting order parameter - $\phi_{0}$: magnetic flux quantum, $\phi_{0} = h / (2e)$ - $H_{c2}$: upper critical field - $T_{c}$: superconducting critical temperature - $T$: temperature - $v_{F}$: Fermi velocity - $h$: Planck constant - $k_{\mathrm{B}}$: Boltzmann constant - $YH_{9}$: yttrium hydride with a stoichiometric ratio of 1:9 - $YD_{9}$: yttrium deuteride with a stoichiometric ratio of 1:9 - GL: Ginzburg–Landau theory - WHH: Werthamer–Helfand–Hohenberg model - BCS: Bardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer theory

premises.premise: The Ginzburg-Landau coherence length $\xi$ of the superconducting order parameter is defined by the relation $\xi = \sqrt{\frac{\phi_{0}}{3\pi^{2}H_{c2}}}$, where $\phi_{0}$ is the magnetic flux quantum and $H_{c2}$ is the upper critical field of the superconductor.

The Ginzburg–Landau (GL) equation provides a valid approximation for the temperature dependence of the upper critical field $H_{c2}(T)$ of a superconductor in the temperature regime close to the critical temperature $T_{c}$, specifically defined by the condition $\frac{T_{c}-T}{T_{c}} \ll 1$. [44]

The Werthamer–Helfand–Hohenberg (WHH) model serves as a theoretical framework to approximate the temperature dependence of the upper critical field $H_{c2}(T)$ across the entire temperature range from $0$ K to the critical temperature $T_{c}$. [45]

In the context of the Bardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer (BCS) theory of superconductivity, the coherence length $\xi$ is related to the Fermi velocity $v_{F}$ and the superconducting critical temperature $T_{c}$ by the expression $\xi = 0.18 \frac{h v_{F}}{k_{\mathrm{B}} T_{c}}$, where $h$ is the Planck constant and $k_{\mathrm{B}}$ is the Boltzmann constant.

premise.title: Scaling Relation for Upper Critical Field

premises.premise: For the yttrium hydride systems $YH_{9}$ and $YD_{9}$ under high pressure, the zero-temperature upper critical field $H_{c2}(0\text{ K})$ is assumed to follow a scaling relation $H_{c2} \sim (T_{c}/v_{F})^{2}$, which, for constant Fermi velocity $v_{F}$, implies that $H_{c2}$ scales with the square of the critical temperature $T_{c}$.
premises.assumption: The onset of the superconducting state (defined as the critical temperature $T_{c}$) in yttrium-hydrogen and yttrium-deuterium samples under an applied external magnetic field is determined experimentally using the criterion of 90% of the electrical resistance observed in the normal metallic state.

For the purpose of comparing the superconducting properties of yttrium hydride ($YH_{9}$) and its deuterated counterpart yttrium deuteride ($YD_{9}$), the Fermi velocity $v_{F}$ is assumed to remain invariant under isotopic substitution of hydrogen with deuterium.
